---
title: "Three years in South Africa"
subtitle: "What moving to Cape Town did to my work, my family, and my sense of where 'the field' is"
description: >
  A personal note on relocating from Belgium to the Cape, building an ML team
  from the southern tip of Africa, and what I'll carry back to Europe.
date: 2026-06-26
categories: [personal, life]
image: cape-town.jpg          # drop in a favourite photo
image-alt: "View over Cape Town"
draft: true   # <- remove this line to publish
---

> **Outline / draft.** This one is personal, so it should be in your voice, 
> I've only left signposts and prompts. Keep it concrete: specific mornings,
> specific people, specific small frustrations.

## Leaving

> Why you left Belgium in 2023, and what the decision felt like at the time.
> One honest sentence about the part that was hard.

## Working from the bottom of the map

> The professional thread: leading BioAI from Cape Town, the time-zone reality,
> the surprise of how *close* the European collaborations stayed, and the ways
> being here changed the work (the Indaba, IndabaX, hackathons across the
> continent, hiring across Cape Town and Kigali).

> A point worth making for the readers who are also researchers: what the
> African ML community looks like from the inside, and why "remote from the
> periphery" turned out to be a strength rather than a compromise.

## Life outside the terminal

> The human stuff. Hout Bay, the mountain, the ocean, the things your kids did
> that they couldn't have done anywhere else. A photo or two:

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![A morning somewhere worth remembering](placeholder-1.jpg){fig-alt="Replace me"}

![Another one](placeholder-2.jpg){fig-alt="Replace me"}
:::

## What I'm taking back

> The relocation to Europe is mid-2027. Close on what stays with you, a habit,
> a perspective on the field, a way of working you didn't have before.
